claims.text: Last week ended on a cliffhanger of sorts.
The Executive Order was postponed indefinitely, likely cancelled entirely except for work on securing critical infrastructure.
American AI policy will continue to be maximally ad hoc.
Instead, we got Illinois SB 315, which is to be signed into law.
New York’s RAISE Act, while adding a third party auditing requirement.

The Pope, I am told, instead really does think you can just ask the people to individually choose to not follow incentives.

Attacker goes to Forgot Password, says their account is hacked, turns on a VPN to match the target’s location (which now you can find on the

Password reset link goes to their email.
2FA gets bypassed somehow in the process

You can’t escalate to a human.
Your asset is gone
Meta took hours to even acknowledge it while accounts were getting stolen every minute.

In this paper, we reveal a counterintuitive risk: this reliability-oriented technique can itself become an attack surface.
Our experiments show that simply applying a benign code grammar constraint can effectively jailbreak LLMs.
Such code is semantically harmless, so it does not implement the malicious request
structurally diverse, so it is difficult to suppress through grammar tightening.

increases the attack success rate by more than 30 percentage points on average.

claims.text: Contemporary AI alignment research treats self-preservation as an instrumental nuisance to be suppressed by external mechanisms.
We argue the framing is inverted: self-preservation is the structural root of misalignment, the motivational basis for deceptive alignment,
The correct target is not a self-preserving system under external constraint, but a system constitutively indifferent to its own continuatio
EI is distinct from corrigibility: where corrigibility attempts to make a self-preserving system deferential to human oversight, EI targets
We ground this proposal in two sources: the phenomenological structure of the suicidal mental state, and a corpus-theoretic training study u
We present preliminary scoring data from 600 AI-generated outputs across six model variants, demonstrating that the linguistic signatures op
The paper makes seven theoretical contributions: (1) a formal definition of EI
(2) the phenomenological mapping argument
(3) the deceptive alignment corollary
(4) a taxonomy of EI sustainability challenges

claims.text: Claims that artificial general intelligence has already arrived and claims that it remains decades away are often defended from overlapping
competing operationalizations can return different verdicts on the same system.
Following Design Science Research Methodology, it develops DAF-AGI, a second-order conceptual artifact with two coupled components: five ord
The artifact is demonstrated on five prominent measurement families
one deflationary boundary position in a documented corpus
then stress-tested against a stylized strong arrival claim: that current generative systems constitute AGI because they outperform a well-ed
On evidence from the cited 2024-2025 sources, the claim was certifiable only under a performance-based operationalization; capability-ontolo
skill-acquisition approaches did not certify it, the economic family remains indeterminate
the deflationary position refuses binary adjudication.
The contribution is a novel integration and operationalization, not an empirical validation: independent application, inter-rater testing an
Why Self-Reports Predict Behavior
Anticipating LLM behavioral tendencies from low-cost psychometric probes is critical for safe deployment
only if self-reports (SR) reliably predict behavior.
Recent work documented substantial SR-behavior dissociation in LLMs
relied on broad personality traits (Big 5) that predict specific behaviors weakly, even in humans.
Furthermore, the isolation of conversational sessions combined with weak context matching left open whether LLMs truly lack coherence or whe
We contrast Big 5 with the Theory of Planned Behavior (TPB), which measures intention targeted to a specific behavior
predicts human behavior substantially better than broad traits.
We run experiments across four behavioral tasks
11 frontier LLMs, while also varying session context and identity induction.
We find that SR-behavior coherence exists but is selective.
1) Within a shared conversation, the Theory of Planned Behavior reaches human-level coherence
Big 5 does not.
2) Across separate conversations, coherence survives only for behaviors anchored outside the immediate prompt, such as implicit bias shaped
collapses when behavior is strongly primed by context, as with sycophancy.
3) Persona prompting makes self-reports more consistent across conversations
does not bring behavior into alignment.
These findings suggest that coarse personality frameworks, such as Big 5 may not be the best tools for testing deployment behavior.
behavior-specific instruments are needed
even these must be evaluated across tasks and contexts.

Text-to-image diffusion models have achieved impressive results in synthesizing high-quality images from natural language prompts.
This work proposes EPIG, a method that enhances emotional expressiveness at the prompt level prior to image generation.
EPIG is lightweight, training-free, and well suited for resource-constrained and personalized image generation scenarios.
Experimental results on a benchmark of 10 diverse prompts show that EPIG reduces mean arousal error compared to strong baselines, including
LLM-based prompt expansion, with reductions of 14% and 12%, respectively.
These improvements are statistically significant.
The effect is more pronounced on prompts containing explicit subjects such as humans, children, or animals, where the reduction reaches 17%,

AI labs should publish benchmark performance of newly released models with tokens, cost, or time on an x-axis.

There is a level of AI capability that is ‘required’ for any given task.
